repository.name: nanoclaw-todo-agent

commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: terminal kill panic and preserve sessions across project switches (#70)

* fix: terminal kill panic and preserve sessions across project switches

- Wrap cleanup_terminal_resources_sync in spawn_blocking to prevent
  tokio runtime panic when blocking on AsyncMutex fields
- Make kill() and kill_all() async to properly handle blocking cleanup
- Remove PTY kill loop from project-switch flow to preserve live sessions
- Add tests for multi-project terminal preservation

Fixes runtime panic: "Cannot block the current thread from within a runtime"
when switching projects with active terminals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove useless assert in test

* fix: ensure kill_all completes before app exits

Use ExitRequestApi.prevent_exit() to block shutdown, run kill_all()
in async task, then call app_handle.exit(0) when cleanup completes.
This ensures PTY children are properly killed before the app exits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: move auto-spawn to background thread to keep ps -w responsive

The on-create hook (e.g. pnpm install) was running synchronously on
the tick thread, freezing the ps --watch UI for the entire duration.
Introduces a spawn_actor following the same pattern as prune_actor,
issue_actor, etc. The tick now sends spawnable issues to the background
thread and drains results on the next tick.

Also adds:
- Spawning worker names shown below the ps table during setup
- WorkerStatus::Initializing variant for future use
- spawn_labels config in jig.toml
- Three new issues (config-show-auto-spawn, worker-initializing-state,
  auto-column-checkmark)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: add 5s timeout + localStorage cleanup to prevent auth loading hang

When a stale Supabase auth token exists in localStorage, getSession()
can hang indefinitely due to Web Locks contention. This caused the
login page to show an infinite spinner.

- Add 5s timeout that clears stale auth keys if getSession() hangs
- Add .catch() on getSession() promise
- Clean up localStorage on signOut to prevent stale tokens
- Add mounted flag to prevent state updates after unmount

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add cache policy, config improvements, and Settings/Launch UI fixes (#185)

## Summary

- **Delegation cache eviction**: Added `cache_max_entries` and
`cache_ttl_seconds` config options with FIFO eviction via `OrderedDict`
and TTL-based expiry
- **Config defaults**: Changed `memory` default to `"dial"`,
`cache_delegations` default to `True`
- **Settings page fix**: Fixed config values not refreshing when
re-entering the Project tab (JSON-based useEffect dependency, controlled
Tabs, query invalidation on tab switch)
- **LaunchDialog improvements**: Added memory field, dropdown for cache
delegations, project-settings-aware defaults for all Advanced fields,
runtime placeholder from role's `default_agent`, wider dialog with
vertical scroll, 2-column grid layout
- **CLI**: Added `--memory`, `--cache-max-entries`,
`--cache-ttl-seconds` flags
- **GUI backend**: Added `_config_to_nested()` helper, `merged_nested`
in project config response, memory override passthrough

## Test plan

- [ ] `cd cli && python -m pytest tests/test_delegation_cache.py -v` —
cache eviction and TTL tests
- [ ] `cd cli && python -m pytest tests/ -v --timeout=30` — full CLI
test suite
- [ ] `cd gui && python -m pytest tests/ -v` — GUI backend tests
- [ ] Verify Settings page: switch between Global/Project tabs, confirm
values refresh correctly
- [ ] Verify LaunchDialog: check defaults match project settings,
runtime placeholder resolves from role's default_agent
- [ ] Verify cache fields show correct placeholder values (not
"(unlimited)" for non-zero values)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

chore: add explicit push rules to agent delivery system

Introduces opt-in push logic to AGENTS.md and DELIVERY_SYSTEM.md.
Default remains commit-only. Push is triggered exclusively by explicit
user phrasing (pushe, committe und pushe, auf GitHub hochladen, etc.).
Defines pre-push check (branch + last commit), post-push output format,
and a two-variant result format (standard vs. push run). DEC-007 updated
to reflect opt-in rather than blanket prohibition.

commit.message:
fix(ci): unify API coverage threshold to 65% (#1626)

## Summary
- CI workflow: 53% → 65%
- Makefile: 58% → 65%
- pyproject.toml: stays at 70% (aspirational target, not lowered per
ratchet rule)

Three inconsistent thresholds created confusion: CI would pass at 53%
while local `pytest` failed at 70%. Now CI and Makefile are aligned at
65%, with pyproject.toml at 70% as the goal.

## Test plan
- [ ] CI green (coverage must be >= 65%)
- [ ] If CI fails due to coverage < 65%, add missing tests or adjust
threshold to current level

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Disable gradient checkpointing + add NaN debug output

Gradient checkpointing recomputes forward during backward, which may
interact badly with complex-valued dropout and sparsemax sort. Disabling
it since B200 has enough VRAM (191.5GB). Also adds debug print showing
actual loss/ce/stability values when NaN is detected.

commit.message:
fix: clamp past departure_time and surface real Google Maps error

- Clamp departure_time to now+60s so Google API accepts requests when
  bucketed time falls in the past (common for "depart now" queries)
- Replace next:{revalidate} with cache:"no-store" — revalidate has
  unpredictable behavior in Next.js 15 Route Handlers
- Surface actual Google error message (status/msg) in 502 response
  so Vercel logs show the real failure reason (e.g. REQUEST_DENIED,
  API not enabled, ZERO_RESULTS)

commit.message:
fix: hold app until Istio proxy ready to prevent startup probe timeout

Istio rewrites HTTP probes to route through its sidecar on port 15020.
Without holdApplicationUntilProxyStarts, kubelet fires the startup probe
before the sidecar is ready, causing context deadline exceeded on
/app-health/tfvisualizer/startupz.

docs: theme registry scope — multi-tenant white-label theming

Technical scope for the Collet Theme Registry:
- Upload API: customers submit token YAML, validated on ingest
- Serve API: CSS served by customer ID with version pinning
- Three implementation paths: serverless (Cloudflare Workers + WASM),
  Axum API server, or git-based (zero infrastructure)
- Version management: immutable history, diff between versions
- Security: structured parsing (no eval), API key auth, tenant isolation
- Dashboard roadmap: live preview with Collet Components

commit.message:
Fix Whisper MLX/CoreML Korean output: wire prompt through and fix v2 language token count

The transcription prompt (e.g. Chinese conditioning text) was read from
UserDefaults but never passed to the Whisper engine, causing the decoder
to lack language conditioning and occasionally drift to Korean output.

Also fix language detection to use 99 language tokens for v1/v2 models
(nMels=80) instead of 100, since token 50358 is <|translate|> in v2,
not a language token.

fix: allow multi-segment extension names (#670)

## Summary

- The CLI's `SCOPED_NAME_PATTERN` regex only allowed two-segment
extension names (`@collective/name`), rejecting hierarchical names like
`@swamp/aws/ec2` or `@swamp/aws/accessanalyzer/analyzer`
- The swamp-club API already supports multi-segment names — this was a
CLI-only restriction that blocked publishing extensions with deeper path
hierarchies
- Updated the regex from `^@[a-z0-9_-]+\/[a-z0-9_-]+$` to
`^@[a-z0-9_-]+\/[a-z0-9_-]+(\/[a-z0-9_-]+)*$` in all 4 locations
(manifest validation, pull, rm, yank commands)
- Added tests for `@swamp/aws/ec2` and
`@swamp/aws/accessanalyzer/analyzer` name formats

## Test Plan

- [x] New unit tests for multi-segment names pass
- [x] All 2788 existing tests pass
- [x] `deno fmt --check` passes
- [x] `deno lint` passes
- [x] Verified swamp-club API already accepts multi-segment names (no
server-side changes needed)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
